steps.title: Reduce the system to one line
steps.content: Multiply the first equation by $5$: $5(7x+4y)=5(3)$ gives $35x+20y=15$, which is identical to the second equation. Both equations describe the same line, $7x+4y=3$.

steps.title: Use Desmos to test the parametrized choices
steps.content: A parametrization works if $7x+4y$ is always $3$. Let $r=[0,1]$ and define $L(x,y)=7x+4y$. Then enter each choice into $L$. The correct choice should output $[3,3]$, meaning both tested $r$-values stay on the line.

steps.title: Confirm choice B
steps.content: Choice B has $x=-\frac{4r}{7}+\frac{3}{7}$ and $y=r$. Substituting into $7x+4y$ gives $7\left(-\frac{4r}{7}+\frac{3}{7}\right)+4r=-4r+3+4r=3$ for every $r$. That matches choice B.
